https://github.com/mapk-amazon/galaxy-k8s-job-retries/commit/c245afab73b2af69b8583349542f33c895f4dfac

Unfortunately, I now have the feeling that it was just an artifact produced by the core issue (https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/490), not the root cause. In my understanding:

- The job gets scheduled
- The job gets updated 2-3 times for processing (potentially pre- and postprocessing)
- The job gets finally updated to scale it down to 0 before it gets deleted

The error message appears when the final "scale to 0" update is too fast and interacts with the previous updates. And this _only_ happens when the job had issues and the scaling to 0 gets triggered earlier than for successful jobs. 

I am atm at a loss what causes the behaviour in the first place.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/comments/2397337270/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/490","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/490/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/490/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/490/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/490","id":2425959625,"node_id":"I_kwDOBCYhWs6QmTTJ","number":490,"title":"Multiple/many parallel jobs lead to "random" failures","user":{"login":"mapk-amazon","id":125464188,"node_id":"U_kgDOB3pufA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/125464188?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon","html_url":"https://github.com/mapk-amazon","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":17,"created_at":"2024-07-23T19:27:13Z","updated_at":"2024-10-14T20:31:32Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"## Setup

The setup is deployed on AWS on EKS: 
- Version k8s: 1.28
- Version Helm Chart: v5.9.0

## Issue 

Galaxy "usually" deploys jobs just fine. We started importing with Batch files into Galaxy and experience random failures of pods. 

## Logs

```
galaxy.jobs.runners.kubernetes ERROR 2024-07-23 15:35:40,109 [pN:job_handler_0,p:8,tN:KubernetesRunner.monitor_thread] No Jobs are available under expected selector app=gxy-galaxy-g674v
galaxy.jobs.runners.kubernetes ERROR 2024-07-23 15:35:40,120 [pN:job_handler_0,p:8,tN:KubernetesRunner.monitor_thread] No Jobs are available under expected selector app=gxy-galaxy-zpgqx
galaxy.jobs.runners.kubernetes ERROR 2024-07-23 15:35:40,130 [pN:job_handler_0,p:8,tN:KubernetesRunner.monitor_thread] No Jobs are available under expected selector app=gxy-galaxy-7kl4g
galaxy.jobs.runners.kubernetes ERROR 2024-07-23 15:35:40,159 [pN:job_handler_0,p:8,tN:KubernetesRunner.monitor_thread] No Jobs are available under expected selector app=gxy-galaxy-g9ts6
```
and 
```
requests.exceptions.HTTPError: 409 Client Error: Conflict for url: [https://172.20.0.1:443/apis/batch/v1/namespaces/galaxy/jobs/gxy-galaxy-f4b62](https://172.20.0.1/apis/batch/v1/namespaces/galaxy/jobs/gxy-galaxy-f4b62)
pykube.exceptions.HTTPError: Operation cannot be fulfilled on jobs.batch "gxy-galaxy-f4b62": the object has been modified; please apply your changes to the latest version and try again
```
In the k8s log we also see that the pods was launched around the time:
```
gxy-galaxy-f4b62-95mlz               0/1     ContainerCreating   0          1s
gxy-galaxy-f4b62-95mlz               1/1     Running             0          4s
gxy-galaxy-f4b62-95mlz               0/1     Completed           0          7s
```


## Ideas/Hypothesis
Current ideas are that the hash (e.g. `f4b62`) has a collision and leads to resource conflicts for the pods and to failures of some jobs. 

Does the team has any experience with it? Any fixes? Thank you :)","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/490/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/490/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/comments/2412205528","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/490#issuecomment-2412205528","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/490","id":2412205528,"node_id":"IC_kwDOBCYhWs6Px1XY","user":{"login":"mapk-amazon","id":125464188,"node_id":"U_kgDOB3pufA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/125464188?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon","html_url":"https://github.com/mapk-amazon","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-10-14T20:31:31Z","updated_at":"2024-10-14T20:31:31Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"**Update** : I believe I know now what is happening. In my understanding the aggressive "retries" are the root cause of the issues. 

The job pod (the one scheduling the pods) shows for failing pods, that "Galaxy" receives twice the information about the pod.
```
DEBUG 2024-10-14 20:12:36,480 [pN:job_handler_0,p:8,tN:KubernetesRunner.monitor_thread] Job id: gxy-galaxy-dkpc5 with k8s id: gxy-galaxy-dkpc5 succeeded
DEBUG 2024-10-14 20:12:38,484 [pN:job_handler_0,p:8,tN:KubernetesRunner.monitor_thread] Job id: gxy-galaxy-dkpc5 with k8s id: gxy-galaxy-dkpc5 succeeded
```
Then it starts cleaning (twice) and one fails, as the other one already deleted/starting deletion. Finally, it shows `tool_stdout` and `tool_stderr` twice:

```
DEBUG 2024-10-14 20:12:54,185 [pN:job_handler_0,p:8,tN:KubernetesRunner.work_thread-1] (3464/gxy-galaxy-dkpc5) tool_stdout: 
DEBUG 2024-10-14 20:12:54,186 [pN:job_handler_0,p:8,tN:KubernetesRunner.work_thread-1] (3464/gxy-galaxy-dkpc5) job_stdout: 
```

```
DEBUG 2024-10-14 20:12:54,186 [pN:job_handler_0,p:8,tN:KubernetesRunner.work_thread-1] (3464/gxy-galaxy-dkpc5) tool_stderr: 
DEBUG 2024-10-14 20:12:54,186 [pN:job_handler_0,p:8,tN:KubernetesRunner.work_thread-1] (3464/gxy-galaxy-dkpc5) job_stderr: Job output not returned from cluster
```

It seems the first job moved the data already and the second did no longer found the file.

The result is a technically successful job (as the container finished), the results were processed successfully once, and the second iteration (the later one) responds with an error and Galaxy believes the job fails.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/comments/2412205528/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"opened","number":19001,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/pulls/19001","id":2125634248,"node_id":"PR_kwDOAdw-Nc5-sprI","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/19001","diff_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/19001.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/19001.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/19001","number":19001,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Fixes random job failures in kubernetes ","user":{"login":"mapk-amazon","id":125464188,"node_id":"U_kgDOB3pufA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/125464188?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon","html_url":"https://github.com/mapk-amazon","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This fix addresses the random crashes of k8s jobs in Galaxy: https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/490 and mentioned therein. The issue is that k8s job status may not be ready, while providing already some information: 
https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.30/#jobstatus-v1-batch

In the previous code `if len(job.obj["status"]) == 0:` checked whether some information is there; if yes, treat it as "final" state of the job and continue processing. In the case that the job status has the field `uncountedTerminatedPods`, k8s is not done with analyzing whether the job failed or succeeded. The code then used this information (for me 0 succeeded, 0 active and 0 failed) and went through the decision tree to decide what to do.  

My suggestion is to instead wait for k8s to determine the status of the terminated pods and only then decide what to do. This reduced the failure rate from 2-5% to 0% :)

## How to test the changes?
- [x] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. The change is difficult to test. It covers an edge where a/ the job is completed, b/ k8s has yet to determine the status of the job. For me 2-5% of jobs fail without the fix.

The setup is deployed on AWS on EKS: 
- Version k8s: 1.28
- Version Helm Chart: v5.9.0

## Issue 

Galaxy "usually" deploys jobs just fine. We started importing with Batch files into Galaxy and experience random failures of pods. 

## Logs

```
galaxy.jobs.runners.kubernetes ERROR 2024-07-23 15:35:40,109 [pN:job_handler_0,p:8,tN:KubernetesRunner.monitor_thread] No Jobs are available under expected selector app=gxy-galaxy-g674v
galaxy.jobs.runners.kubernetes ERROR 2024-07-23 15:35:40,120 [pN:job_handler_0,p:8,tN:KubernetesRunner.monitor_thread] No Jobs are available under expected selector app=gxy-galaxy-zpgqx
galaxy.jobs.runners.kubernetes ERROR 2024-07-23 15:35:40,130 [pN:job_handler_0,p:8,tN:KubernetesRunner.monitor_thread] No Jobs are available under expected selector app=gxy-galaxy-7kl4g
galaxy.jobs.runners.kubernetes ERROR 2024-07-23 15:35:40,159 [pN:job_handler_0,p:8,tN:KubernetesRunner.monitor_thread] No Jobs are available under expected selector app=gxy-galaxy-g9ts6
```
and 
```
requests.exceptions.HTTPError: 409 Client Error: Conflict for url: [https://172.20.0.1:443/apis/batch/v1/namespaces/galaxy/jobs/gxy-galaxy-f4b62](https://172.20.0.1/apis/batch/v1/namespaces/galaxy/jobs/gxy-galaxy-f4b62)
pykube.exceptions.HTTPError: Operation cannot be fulfilled on jobs.batch "gxy-galaxy-f4b62": the object has been modified; please apply your changes to the latest version and try again
```
In the k8s log we also see that the pods was launched around the time:
```
gxy-galaxy-f4b62-95mlz               0/1     ContainerCreating   0          1s
gxy-galaxy-f4b62-95mlz               1/1     Running             0          4s
gxy-galaxy-f4b62-95mlz               0/1     Completed           0          7s
```


## Ideas/Hypothesis
Current ideas are that the hash (e.g. `f4b62`) has a collision and leads to resource conflicts for the pods and to failures of some jobs. 

Does the team has any experience with it? Any fixes? ","user":{"login":"mapk-amazon","id":125464188,"node_id":"U_kgDOB3pufA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/125464188?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon","html_url":"https://github.com/mapk-amazon","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This fix addresses the random crashes of k8s jobs in Galaxy: https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/490 and mentioned therein. The issue is that k8s job status may not be ready, while providing already some information: 
https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.30/#jobstatus-v1-batch

In the previous code `if len(job.obj["status"]) == 0:` checked whether some information is there; if yes, treat it as "final" state of the job and continue processing. In the case that the job status has the field `uncountedTerminatedPods`, k8s is not done with analyzing whether the job failed or succeeded. The code then used this information (for me 0 succeeded, 0 active and 0 failed) and went through the decision tree to decide what to do.  

My suggestion is to instead wait for k8s to determine the status of the terminated pods and only then decide what to do. This reduced the failure rate from 2-5% to 0% :)

## How to test the changes?
- [x] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. The change is difficult to test. It covers an edge where a/ the job is completed, b/ k8s has yet to determine the status of the job. For me 2-5% of jobs fail without the fix.

","user":{"login":"mapk-amazon","id":125464188,"node_id":"U_kgDOB3pufA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/125464188?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon","html_url":"https://github.com/mapk-amazon","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This fix addresses the random crashes of k8s jobs in Galaxy: https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/490 and mentioned therein. The issue is that k8s job status may not be ready, while providing already some information: 
https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.30/#jobstatus-v1-batch

In the previous code `if len(job.obj["status"]) == 0:` checked whether some information is there; if yes, treat it as "final" state of the job and continue processing. In the case that the job status has the field `uncountedTerminatedPods`, k8s is not done with analyzing whether the job failed or succeeded. The code then used this information (for me 0 succeeded, 0 active and 0 failed) and went through the decision tree to decide what to do.  

My suggestion is to instead wait for k8s to determine the status of the terminated pods and only then decide what to do. This reduced the failure rate from 2-5% to 0% :)

## How to test the changes?
- [x] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. The change is difficult to test. It covers an edge where a/ the job is completed, b/ k8s has yet to determine the status of the job. For me 2-5% of jobs fail without the fix.

","user":{"login":"mapk-amazon","id":125464188,"node_id":"U_kgDOB3pufA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/125464188?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon","html_url":"https://github.com/mapk-amazon","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":180024669,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxODAwMjQ2Njk=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/labels/kind/bug","name":"kind/bug","color":"84b6eb","default":false,"description":null},{"id":276558748,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwyNzY1NTg3NDg=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/labels/area/jobs","name":"area/jobs","color":"006b75","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/milestones/28","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/milestone/28","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/milestones/28/labels","id":11051799,"node_id":"MI_kwDOAdw-Nc4AqKMX","number":28,"title":"24.2","description":"","creator":{"login":"jdavcs","id":6132258,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjYxMzIyNTg=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/6132258?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs","html_url":"https://github.com/jdavcs","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"open_issues":66,"closed_issues":179,"state":"open","created_at":"2024-05-14T14:03:11Z","updated_at":"2024-10-17T22:31:27Z","due_on":null,"closed_at":null},"comments":4,"created_at":"2024-10-15T15:47:50Z","updated_at":"2024-10-18T11:32:22Z","closed_at":"2024-10-17T12:58:08Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/pulls/19001","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/19001","diff_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/19001.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/19001.patch","merged_at":"2024-10-17T12:58:08Z"},"body":"This fix addresses the random crashes of k8s jobs in Galaxy: https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/490 and mentioned therein. The issue is that k8s job status may not be ready, while providing already some information: 
https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.30/#jobstatus-v1-batch

In the previous code `if len(job.obj["status"]) == 0:` checked whether some information is there; if yes, treat it as "final" state of the job and continue processing. In the case that the job status has the field `uncountedTerminatedPods`, k8s is not done with analyzing whether the job failed or succeeded. The code then used this information (for me 0 succeeded, 0 active and 0 failed) and went through the decision tree to decide what to do.  

My suggestion is to instead wait for k8s to determine the status of the terminated pods and only then decide what to do. This reduced the failure rate from 2-5% to 0% :)

## How to test the changes?
- [x] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. The change is difficult to test. It covers an edge where a/ the job is completed, b/ k8s has yet to determine the status of the job. For me 2-5% of jobs fail without the fix.

The setup is deployed on AWS on EKS: 
- Version k8s: 1.28
- Version Helm Chart: v5.9.0

## Issue 

Galaxy "usually" deploys jobs just fine. We started importing with Batch files into Galaxy and experience random failures of pods. 

## Logs

```
galaxy.jobs.runners.kubernetes ERROR 2024-07-23 15:35:40,109 [pN:job_handler_0,p:8,tN:KubernetesRunner.monitor_thread] No Jobs are available under expected selector app=gxy-galaxy-g674v
galaxy.jobs.runners.kubernetes ERROR 2024-07-23 15:35:40,120 [pN:job_handler_0,p:8,tN:KubernetesRunner.monitor_thread] No Jobs are available under expected selector app=gxy-galaxy-zpgqx
galaxy.jobs.runners.kubernetes ERROR 2024-07-23 15:35:40,130 [pN:job_handler_0,p:8,tN:KubernetesRunner.monitor_thread] No Jobs are available under expected selector app=gxy-galaxy-7kl4g
galaxy.jobs.runners.kubernetes ERROR 2024-07-23 15:35:40,159 [pN:job_handler_0,p:8,tN:KubernetesRunner.monitor_thread] No Jobs are available under expected selector app=gxy-galaxy-g9ts6
```
and 
```
requests.exceptions.HTTPError: 409 Client Error: Conflict for url: [https://172.20.0.1:443/apis/batch/v1/namespaces/galaxy/jobs/gxy-galaxy-f4b62](https://172.20.0.1/apis/batch/v1/namespaces/galaxy/jobs/gxy-galaxy-f4b62)
pykube.exceptions.HTTPError: Operation cannot be fulfilled on jobs.batch "gxy-galaxy-f4b62": the object has been modified; please apply your changes to the latest version and try again
```
In the k8s log we also see that the pods was launched around the time:
```
gxy-galaxy-f4b62-95mlz               0/1     ContainerCreating   0          1s
gxy-galaxy-f4b62-95mlz               1/1     Running             0          4s
gxy-galaxy-f4b62-95mlz               0/1     Completed           0          7s
```


## Ideas/Hypothesis
Current ideas are that the hash (e.g. `f4b62`) has a collision and leads to resource conflicts for the pods and to failures of some jobs. 

Does the team has any experience with it? Any fixes? The setup is deployed on AWS on EKS: 
- Version k8s: 1.28
- Version Helm Chart: v5.9.0

## Issue 

Galaxy "usually" deploys jobs just fine. We started importing with Batch files into Galaxy and experience random failures of pods. 

## Logs

```
galaxy.jobs.runners.kubernetes ERROR 2024-07-23 15:35:40,109 [pN:job_handler_0,p:8,tN:KubernetesRunner.monitor_thread] No Jobs are available under expected selector app=gxy-galaxy-g674v
galaxy.jobs.runners.kubernetes ERROR 2024-07-23 15:35:40,120 [pN:job_handler_0,p:8,tN:KubernetesRunner.monitor_thread] No Jobs are available under expected selector app=gxy-galaxy-zpgqx

```
and 
```
requests.exceptions.HTTPError: 409 Client Error: Conflict for url: [https://172.20.0.1:443/apis/batch/v1/namespaces/galaxy/jobs/gxy-galaxy-f4b62](https://172.20.0.1/apis/batch/v1/namespaces/galaxy/jobs/gxy-galaxy-f4b62)
pykube.exceptions.HTTPError: Operation cannot be fulfilled on jobs.batch "gxy-galaxy-f4b62": the object has been modified; please apply your changes to the latest version and try again
```
In the k8s log we also see that the pods was launched around the time:
```
gxy-galaxy-f4b62-95mlz               0/1     ContainerCreating   0          1s
gxy-galaxy-f4b62-95mlz               1/1     Running             0          4s
gxy-galaxy-f4b62-95mlz               0/1     Completed           0          7s
```


## Ideas/Hypothesis
Current ideas are that the hash (e.g. `f4b62`) has a collision and leads to resource conflicts for the pods and to failures of some jobs. 

Does the team has any experience with it? Any fixes? The setup is deployed on AWS on EKS: 
- Version k8s: 1.28
- Version Helm Chart: v5.9.0

## Issue 

Galaxy "usually" deploys jobs just fine. We started importing with Batch files into Galaxy and experience random failures of pods. 

## Logs

```
galaxy.jobs.runners.kubernetes ERROR 2024-07-23 15:35:40,109 [pN:job_handler_0,p:8,tN:KubernetesRunner.monitor_thread] No Jobs are available under expected selector app=gxy-galaxy-g674v
galaxy.jobs.runners.kubernetes ERROR 2024-07-23 15:35:40,120 [pN:job_handler_0,p:8,tN:KubernetesRunner.monitor_thread] No Jobs are available under expected selector app=gxy-galaxy-zpgqx
galaxy.jobs.runners.kubernetes ERROR 2024-07-23 15:35:40,130 [pN:job_handler_0,p:8,tN:KubernetesRunner.monitor_thread] No Jobs are available under expected selector app=gxy-galaxy-7kl4g
galaxy.jobs.runners.kubernetes ERROR 2024-07-23 15:35:40,159 [pN:job_handler_0,p:8,tN:KubernetesRunner.monitor_thread] No Jobs are available under expected selector app=gxy-galaxy-g9ts6
```
and 
```
requests.exceptions.HTTPError: 409 Client Error: Conflict for url: [https://172.20.0.1:443/apis/batch/v1/namespaces/galaxy/jobs/gxy-galaxy-f4b62](https://172.20.0.1/apis/batch/v1/namespaces/galaxy/jobs/gxy-galaxy-f4b62)
pykube.exceptions.HTTPError: Operation cannot be fulfilled on jobs.batch "gxy-galaxy-f4b62": the object has been modified; please apply your changes to the latest version and try again
```
In the k8s log we also see that the pods was launched around the time:
```
gxy-galaxy-f4b62-95mlz               0/1     ContainerCreating   0          1s
gxy-galaxy-f4b62-95mlz               1/1     Running             0          4s
gxy-galaxy-f4b62-95mlz               0/1     Completed           0          7s
```


## Ideas/Hypothesis
Current ideas are that the hash (e.g. `f4b62`) has a collision and leads to resource conflicts for the pods and to failures of some jobs. 

Does the team has any experience with it? Any fixes? job priorityClass value is set here to -1000: https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/blob/4e12bbb7caee93cd05d997ba93ff33e7a596c74e/galaxy/templates/priorityclass-job.yaml#L8

As the value is very low, k8s does not start new nodes to work these jobs. 

*Use Case*: k8s uses two node groups: one "exclusively" for jobs and one for the web server and other non-performance intensive pods. The job node group consists of powerful machines, which should scale to 0 when no job is running.  Currently, k8s won't spin those machines up, as the priority of the jobs is too low.

*Suggestion* Expose this as variable to allow customizations to allow the increase of the priority for such a use case.

Thank you!","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/450/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/450/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

When I do, https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/blob/master/galaxy/templates/hook-cvmfs-fix.yaml this hook starts up and continues to wait for some cvmfs pods (ironically to kill them). As there is no pod, it runs indefinitely.

```
refdata.enabled: false
refdata.type: s3sci
s3csi.deploy: false
cvmfs.deploy: false
cvmfs.enabled: false
```

*Expected behaviour*:
If no cvmfs is used, then the pod for fixing cvmfs does not run. _Alternatively_ after no pod is found for 1-2 minutes, the pod finishes up.

Thank you!","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/439/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/439/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16373","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16373/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16373/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16373/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16373","id":1791064892,"node_id":"I_kwDOAdw-Nc5qwXs8","number":16373,"title":"Azure AD OIDC is missing tenant","user":{"login":"mapk-amazon","id":125464188,"node_id":"U_kgDOB3pufA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/125464188?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon","html_url":"https://github.com/mapk-amazon","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2023-07-06T08:25:12Z","updated_at":"2023-07-06T08:25:12Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the bug**
Adding Azure as OIDC w/ according to the samle `oidc_backend_config.xml.sample` (https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/blob/dev/lib/galaxy/config/sample/oidc_backends_config.xml.sample) leads to the 

AADSTS900023 Error: Specified tenant identifier 'none' is neither a valid DNS name, nor a valid external domain.

The tenant id is provided implicitly in the api url. This url is however not used. Instead it is constructed automatically via the `<tenant_id>` field and the default url. 

Quick fix: Remove the `<api_url>` from the sample config and replace it with `<tenant_id>`. 

As far as I can tell the used python library does not use `api_irl` for azure: 
https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Apython-social-auth%2Fsocial-core+api_url&type=code

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: 23.1
Commit: https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/commit/d9a84dff2cbd9b18dbbf716f52c95fb54c3c6163

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Set up Azure AD in MS Azure Cloud
2. Set up Azure AD Integration via `galaxy.yml` and `oidc_backend_config.xml.sample` according to sample files and infos provided to you by MS.
3. Click on Login and choose Azure AD
4. See error

**Expected behavior**
Forwarded to login page.

**Screenshots**
![image (3)](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/assets/125464188/75b0fb4a-b3f3-4e6d-94e4-327e25da52f6)

**Additional context**

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/431","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/431/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/431/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/431/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/431","id":1702588970,"node_id":"I_kwDOBCYhWs5le3Iq","number":431,"title":"Requirements not installed before the start of web server","user":{"login":"mapk-amazon","id":125464188,"node_id":"U_kgDOB3pufA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/125464188?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon","html_url":"https://github.com/mapk-amazon","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mapk-amazon/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2023-05-09T18:53:47Z","updated_at":"2023-05-09T18:53:47Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"For me the galaxy deployment does not check and install needed dependencies before the start of the web server.

Concrete: Configuring `auth_conf.xml` with `<type>ldap3</type>` lets Galaxy recognize that `python-ldap3` is a dependency. This works when `run.sh` is triggered. It identifies the dependency and downloads the library.

In the helm chart, the server boots up and does not download this library and hence Active Directory does not work.

Current workaround: Installing the library manually via `extraInitCommands`","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/431/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy-helm/issues/431/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}
